title: Signal-to-Noise Ratio
text: A **signal-to-noise ratio** compares useful signal strength with background interference. In telecom research, it helps explain why a network may have coverage but still deliver weak voice or data quality.

title: Spectrum Refarming
text: **Spectrum refarming** means reallocating older frequency bands to newer network technologies. Operators often use this approach to improve 4G and 5G capacity without waiting for entirely new spectrum awards.

title: Tower Backhaul
text: Tower **backhaul** is the transport link between a radio site and the core network. It may use fiber, microwave, or satellite depending on terrain, cost, and the required traffic capacity.

title: Legacy vs. New Services
text: **Legacy services** cover voice, SMS, and basic connectivity. **New services** include cloud, AI, cybersecurity, IoT, fintech, and enterprise digital offerings. MENA operators are shifting revenue mix toward new services through 2030.

title: Programmable Networks
text: **Programmable networks** use cloud-native architectures, network virtualization, and 5G standalone deployments to turn connectivity into an intelligent, low-latency platform for enterprise and consumer services.

title: Techco Transformation
text: A **techco** shift moves telcos from asset-heavy infrastructure owners to platform orchestrators. Operators divest passive assets and reinvest in computing, data-intensive applications, and enterprise digitization.
